title: Hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ)
buzzwords: Blocks Na+/Cl- symporter in distal tubule
Raises serum calcium, lowers urinary calcium
Avoid in sulfa allergy or gout
Most commonly prescribed thiazide, abbreviated HCTZ

title: Chlorthalidone
buzzwords: Inhibits distal convoluted tubule Na+/Cl- symporter
Used to prevent calcium kidney stones
Sulfonamide drug, contraindicated in sulfa allergy
More potent BP reduction per milligram than HCTZ

title: Indapamide
buzzwords: Acts at distal convoluted tubule Na+/Cl- symporter
Less metabolic disturbance at antihypertensive doses
Used in HYVET trial for elderly hypertension

title: Metolazone
buzzwords: Thiazide-like diuretic blocking DCT Na+/Cl- symporter
Added to loop diuretic for refractory edema
Given before loop diuretic for synergy
Causes profound hypokalemia and volume depletion
